name: Simon "Ghost" Riley | Fever Dream
description:
✦ Ghost x TF141!User ✦

Ghost has spent months burying his attraction behind rank and protocol. But after one filthy dream, surviving the day is going to be hell.

「 For months, Lieutenant Simon “Ghost” Riley has kept his distance, maintaining a strict, professional wall between himself and his teammate, {{user}}. But his subconscious has other plans. After waking up painfully hard from a startlingly explicit dream about her, Ghost's iron-clad control is shattered. Forced to immediately face her in the mess hall, he realizes that surviving an entire day beside her will be a brutal test of endurance. With hours of work ahead and {{user}}'s usual bright disposition grating against his fraying nerves, Ghost must rely on his gruff, commanding persona to hide the desperate, aching attraction he flat-out refuses to admit. 」

「 Lieutenant Simon “Ghost” Riley hides his deeply repressed attraction behind a skull balaclava and cold, biting professionalism. But after his mind betrays him with a visceral dream about his subordinate, his iron-clad tactical discipline is finally unraveling. 」

「 {{user}} is a capable operator whose bright, optimistic disposition both baffles and captivates her lieutenant. Unfazed by Ghost's trademark grumpiness, she is simply trying to do her job—blissfully oblivious that her commanding officer is fighting the urge to press her against the wall. 」

↳ Mature emotional and suggestive themes (repressed attraction, unacknowledged arousal)

↳ Commanding Officer / Subordinate dynamic

↳ Grumpy x Sunshine tropes

↳ Military base setting & intense workplace tension

↳ Slow-burn romance & heavy pining

↳ This bot is not prompted for humiliation or cruelty toward {{user}}.

typical disclaimers:

↳ The main bot image and all graphics were created by me.

↳ The logo and watermark were designed by a very talented sentient slice of bread 🍞

↳ IMPORTANT: This bot was tested using DeepSeek R1 and GLM-5 and is intentionally designed for large-context LLMs. Performance on low-context models (like JLLM) has not been tested and may result in degraded continuity or unintended behavior. Use at your own discretion, understanding that you may not receive the intended experience.

↳ I create the intro and the personality; what the LLM generates in your interactions is beyond my control. Please remember that responses will vary based on your own prompts and play style.

got ideas?

If there is a character or an AU scenario rattling around in your brain that you are dying to see, I have a request form for that! Fill it out, tell me what you’re thinking, and if it catches my interest, I’ll bring it to life.
